blurb:
There's something about a cat named Sebastian. It sounds like he already owns a velvet chair somewhere and is just waiting for the right house to put it in. He's an adult male domestic shorthair at Good Mews in Marietta, and while his online profile is still being built out, the photo tells you plenty.

Adult cats at Good Mews have been living in a cage-free environment, meaning Sebastian is already used to couches, foot traffic, and the general rhythm of a home. He's not going to need weeks to figure out what a couch is. He already knows.

If you've been thinking about adding a cat and don't want the chaos of a kitten, Sebastian is the kind of guy worth going to meet in person. Good Mews is on Robinson Road in Marietta. You can call or email to get the process started.
shelter_hours: Please contact shelter for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Positioned as the ideal low-maintenance adult cat for readers who want companionship without kitten chaos
• Personality-rich name lends itself to a fun, shareable newsletter headline with broad reader appeal
• Profile still being built suggests relatively recent intake, limiting urgency compared to longer-waiting peers

blurb:
Panda is an adult female mixed breed, and yes, the name fits. Her profile is new and still being filled in, so the rescue doesn't have a full write-up on her yet, but her photo is doing a lot of the talking.

She looks like a dog who has seen a little of life and come out the other side calm about it. Adult female mixed breeds are often some of the most overlooked dogs in rescue and also some of the easiest to bring home. They've usually settled into who they are, which makes the adjustment period a lot smoother than people expect.

Panda is listed through Rescue Me GA in the Dunwoody area. Reach out through her Petfinder page to get more details on her background, what she's like with people, and how to set up a meeting. Sometimes the ones with the thinnest profiles move the fastest once someone actually goes to meet them.
scoring_notes:
• Memorable name with visual payoff is an instant reader hook that drives engagement
• "Seen a little of life" framing adds quiet emotional depth without requiring a detailed backstory
• Profile described as new, suggesting recent intake and a shorter shelter stay so far

blurb:
Rudolph is an adult male Poodle, and he's available right now through Rescue Me GA in Dunwoody. His full bio hasn't been written yet, so we're working with what we've got, but Poodles are worth paying attention to regardless. They're sharp, they're loyal, and they tend to bond hard with the right person.

What the photo shows is a dog with some personality behind the eyes. Not the kind of dog who fades into the background. Adult Poodles and Poodle types are often already past the chewing-everything phase and ready to just be someone's dog, which is a pretty great place to start.

If you've been thinking about a smaller, lower-shedding dog and keep putting it off, Rudolph is a decent reason to stop waiting. Reach out through his Petfinder listing to ask about his temperament and set up a time to meet him. Rescue Me GA will have the details.
scoring_notes:
• Poodle breed recognition plus low-shedding appeal targets a wide and motivated adopter demographic
• "Stop putting it off" call-to-action implies he has been waiting and builds quiet urgency for readers
• Adult purebred Poodle in rescue is an underdog story that resonates and drives newsletter clicks

blurb:
Eugene doesn't have a full bio yet, but one look at this guy and you already know he's got something going on. He's an adult male domestic shorthair, and based on his photo alone, he has that calm, settled energy that adult cats tend to carry — none of the chaotic kitten stuff, just a cat who knows what he wants and is ready to be somewhere permanent.

Adult cats like Eugene often get passed over because people head straight for the kittens. That's a mistake. Adult cats skip the destructive phase, they're easier to read, and they tend to bond deeply once they're in a home they trust. Eugene is ready for that.

His bio is still being built, so the best way to learn more about him is to reach out to Three Paws Rescue directly through his Petfinder listing. They'll be able to tell you about his personality and what kind of home he'd do best in.
scoring_notes:
• Adult cat with calm, settled energy appeals to readers seeking low-maintenance companionship
• Thin bio limits narrative pull, but the "knows what he wants" framing gives editors a hook
• No urgency signals in the blurb; shelter time is ambiguous and likely modest

blurb:
Otis is an adult mixed breed dog available through Our Pals Place in Marietta, and he's got the kind of name that suggests a dog who takes life at a reasonable pace and would like a yard to hang out in.

His bio hasn't been written up yet — he's new to the rescue and the details are still coming — but mixed breeds tend to be full of personality once they land somewhere comfortable, and Otis looks like he's ready for exactly that. Sometimes the dogs without a long write-up are the ones that surprise you most when you go meet them in person.

If you're open to an adult dog and want to find out who Otis actually is, the best next step is reaching out to Our Pals Place through his Petfinder listing. They're based in Marietta, so he's right in the neighborhood.
scoring_notes:
• Mixed breed framing as a personality-packed surprise is a solid angle but lacks the breed-specific appeal that drives quick adoptions
• 'Surprise when you meet him' narrative is engaging but thin without any concrete details to anchor reader interest
• Described as new with details still coming, indicating recent intake and the lowest urgency signal among all six candidates

blurb:
His name is Goober, and honestly, that alone tells you something. Someone looked at this chocolate Lab and thought: yes, that is a Goober. They were not wrong.

Goober doesn't have a full bio written up yet — he's new to Our Pals Place and his profile is still being built — but one look at that face and you already know what you're dealing with. A big, soft, brown Lab who probably wants to be wherever you are and is going to take up more couch space than you planned for. That's the deal with chocolate Labs. You already know this going in.

If you've been thinking about adding a dog to the house and a grown Lab sounds like your speed, Goober is worth going to meet. Our Pals Place is a Marietta-based rescue — reach out through their Petfinder page to get the inquiry process started.
scoring_notes:
• Chocolate Lab with a goofy name is arguably the highest broad-appeal adoption candidate in this entire batch
• Name alone generates an emotional, shareable moment that newsletter readers will forward to friends
• Described as new to the rescue with a profile still being built, suggesting very short shelter time and low urgency score

blurb:
Calicos have a reputation, and Jane is here to either prove it or completely defy it — you'll have to meet her to find out. She's an adult female calico shorthair, and if you know anything about calicos, you know they tend to have a lot of opinions and are not shy about sharing them. That's not a bad thing. It just means you're getting a cat with a real personality, not a decorative one.

Being an adult works in her favor. She's past the unpredictable phase, she knows what she likes, and she's ready to settle into a home that suits her. Adult cats read the room faster than kittens do, and they tend to attach more deliberately once they decide you're worth their time.

Jane doesn't have a full profile posted yet, so her specific personality details are still coming. Reach out to Patty's Kitties through her Petfinder listing to ask about her temperament and what kind of household she'd thrive in. They're based in Atlanta.
scoring_notes:
• Calico personality framing creates genuine curiosity and invites readers to "find out for themselves"
• Adult female with strong opinions is a memorable character in a sea of generic shelter profiles
• Incomplete profile with adult status hints at a longer wait, though no explicit time signals present

blurb:
The name alone should make you curious. Beignet is an adult female cat at Good Mews, and if she's half as sweet as what she's named after, she's worth the drive out to Marietta.

Her full bio isn't built out yet, so this is based on what's available and what you can see: she's a domestic shorthair with a look that says she's got opinions and isn't afraid to share them. She's been around Good Mews long enough to know the routine, which means she's calm, shelter-adjusted, and ready to actually settle into a real home.

Good Mews is a cage-free, no-kill shelter, so Beignet has been living in a real house environment, not a cage. That matters. Cats from cage-free rescues tend to transition into homes much more smoothly. Stop by in person or reach out to start the process. She's waiting.
shelter_hours: Please contact shelter for current hours
scoring_notes:

blurb:
There's a lot going on with Mandy, and all of it is good. She's a young female Maine Coon mix, which means she's going to be bigger than your average cat, fluffier than expected, and probably a lot more interactive. Maine Coons aren't your typical "leave me alone" cats. They follow you around. They want to know what you're doing. They're basically dogs who use a litter box.

She's young, so she's got energy and curiosity to spare. If you want a cat who's going to grow into a genuinely big personality — and a genuinely big cat — Mandy is worth a serious look. The medium-hair coat is going to need occasional brushing, but that's a pretty reasonable trade for what you're getting.

Mandy's bio is still light on details, so reach out to Patty's Kitties through her Petfinder listing to find out more about her personality and what kind of home she's being matched to. They're based in Atlanta.
scoring_notes:
• Maine Coon mix is a high-appeal breed with broad reader recognition and strong visual impact
• Dog-like personality angle is a proven newsletter hook that converts casual readers into inquiries
• Young cat with no urgency flags suggests a relatively recent intake and shorter wait

blurb:
Midna is named after a character who starts out a little mysterious and ends up being the best part of the whole story. Whether that was intentional or not, it fits. She's an adult female domestic shorthair at Good Mews, and her full profile is still being filled in, but she's available now and worth knowing about.

She's been living in Good Mews' cage-free environment, which means she's used to sharing space with other cats and navigating a home setup already. Adult cats from cage-free rescues skip a lot of the adjustment period that comes with shelter animals. What you see is pretty much what you get.

If you want to meet her before her internet fame catches up, now's the time. Good Mews is on Robinson Road in Marietta. Reach out by phone or email to set up a visit.

blurb:
Harvey is an adult mixed breed with the kind of face that makes you stop scrolling. His bio is still being built, so we don't have a full rundown on his personality yet, but what we do know is that he's available, he's handsome, and he's waiting.

What the photo shows is a dog who looks steady and calm. Not frantic, not bouncing. Just a guy sitting there like he's got somewhere to be and hopes it's your house. Mixed breeds like Harvey tend to be adaptable, and adult dogs skip the chaos of puppyhood, which is worth a lot.

If you're curious, the best move is to reach out to Rescue Me GA directly through the Petfinder link and ask about him. They'll have more details on his temperament, background, and what kind of home he'd do best in. Don't sleep on this one just because his profile is thin. That's usually just a timing thing, not a Harvey thing.
scoring_notes:
• Stop-scrolling face combined with calm adult energy is a strong visual and emotional pairing for print
• "Waiting" language and thin profile signal he may be overlooked, creating meaningful reader urgency
• Direct appeal to readers not to sleep on him suggests he has been available longer than typical

blurb:

Junior's profile is still being filled out, so details on his personality and history are limited for now. Best move is to reach out to Our Pals Place directly through their Petfinder page and ask about him. Marietta-based rescue, so he's close.
